steps.title: Mark the angle relationships
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: triangle sums of $180^\circ$ and supplementary angles on straight lines.

steps.title: Find the third angle in triangle QXS
steps.content:
In triangle $QXS$, the given angles are $\angle SQX = 48^\circ$ and $\angle SXQ = 86^\circ$. Using the triangle angle sum:
$$\angle QSX = 180^\circ - 48^\circ - 86^\circ = 46^\circ$$
Since $W$ lies on segment $SX$, this same angle applies to ray $SW$.

steps.title: Use a linear pair at W to find ‚à†RWS
steps.content:
At point $W$, lines $RU$ and $SX$ intersect. We are given $\angle SWU = 85^\circ$. Since rays $WR$ and $WU$ point in opposite directions on line $RU$, $\angle RWS$ and $\angle SWU$ form a linear pair:
$$\angle RWS + \angle SWU = 180^\circ$$
$$\angle RWS = 180^\circ - 85^\circ = 95^\circ$$

steps.title: Find ‚à†TRU using triangle RWS
steps.content:
In triangle $RWS$, we now have $\angle RWS = 95^\circ$ and $\angle WSR = 46^\circ$. Use the triangle angle sum:
$$\angle WRS = 180^\circ - 95^\circ - 46^\circ = 39^\circ$$
Because $W$ lies on line $RU$ and $S$ and $T$ lie on line $PV$, this gives $\angle TRU = 39^\circ$.

steps.title: Use ‚à†VTU to finish
steps.content:
Since $S$, $T$, and $V$ lie on the same line, $\angle STU$ and $\angle VTU$ are supplementary:
$$\angle STU = 180^\circ - 162^\circ = 18^\circ$$
Now use triangle $RTU$:
$$\angle TUR = 180^\circ - 39^\circ - 18^\circ = 123^\circ$$
The answer is 123.
